text: You opened, clarified, and closed a professional call with a counterpart.
Today, before any call or meeting, comes the small talk that builds the relationship.

Hi, I don't think we have met. I'm Simone. Is this your first time at this conference?
Hi Simone, I'm Daniel. Yes, it is. Thanks for helping me break the ice. What kind of law do you work in?
I work on cross-border deals. How about you? It would be nice to get to know what you do.
Same field, actually, so we have a lot in common. I really liked the point you brought up during the panel.
Thank you. Would you like to catch up over lunch and talk more about it?
I would love to. Let's swap numbers so we can keep in touch after the conference too.
Hi, I don't think we have met. I'm Simone.
It looks like we have a lot in common.
Would you like to catch up over lunch?
Let's keep in touch. Here is my card.

Hi Simone, this is Daniel Brooks. We met for a minute at the legal conference this morning, near the coffee table, but we did not really get to know each other. I wanted to break the ice properly, so I am leaving you this quick voice note. It turns out we have a lot in common: you work on cross-border deals, and so do I. During the panel, you brought up a great point about contract risk, and I would love to catch up and hear more. There is a networking lunch tomorrow at one o'clock. If you are free, it would be a perfect chance to talk. Either way, let us keep in touch. Here is my number. Talk soon, and enjoy the rest of the conference.

Hi, this is Simone. I am leaving a quick note for my team after two days at the legal conference. Honestly, networking felt hard at first, but a few simple things really helped. First, I learned to break the ice with an easy question instead of talking about work right away. A good conversation starter, like asking what brought someone to the event, opens the door fast. Second, I tried to find something in common before bringing up business, and people relaxed. Third, when a chat was going well, I asked to catch up later and we agreed to keep in touch. I met three lawyers who work on similar deals. I will share their contacts this afternoon, so we can wrap up our networking list by Friday. Have a great day.

Simone arrives at the legal conference and feels nervous about networking. Daniel breaks the ice with a friendly question at the coffee break. They find they have a lot in common and talk about their work. Simone brings up a point from the panel, and they agree to catch up at lunch. Before they leave, they exchange numbers and promise to keep in touch.
